channel.title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
channel.description: Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

image.title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
image.description: Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

item.title: US sanctions elected Hezbollah MPs and Lebanese security officials
item.description: Hezbollah says the sanctions will have 'absolutely no effect' on its strategy amid ongoing Israeli attacks on Lebanon.

item.title: Fans create AI-generated team songs ahead of World Cup
item.description: Fan-made football anthems are raking in millions of plays on social media, rivalling FIFA commissions from musicians.

item.title: Ebola tensions rise as treatment centre torched in DR Congo’s Ituri
item.description: Residents in DRC torch facility over burial disputes, spotlighting challenges in tackling Ebola outbreak.

item.title: Five key takeaways from Democrats’ autopsy report on Kamala Harris’s loss
item.description: Riddled with mistakes and missing sections, 192-page document on 2024 election loss to Trump fails to mention Gaza.

item.title: Trump shifts between diplomacy and threats in Iran standoff
item.description: Supporters say unpredictable Trump approach deal-making strength, others see reflection of strategic dilemma.

item.title: Do aid flotillas make a difference for Palestinians under Israeli blockade?
item.description: Israeli minister's mistreatment of abducted activists sparks global condemnation.

item.title: Is Islamophobia being re-packaged as feminism?
item.description: A group of female activists at a far-right rally in the UK have sparked a backlash.

item.title: UK launches ‘Great British Summer Savings’ to ease family costs
item.description: Families to benefit from reduced VAT to help lower costs at theme parks, theatres, zoos, and museums.

item.title: Iran’s World Cup hopes hit US visa hurdles
item.description: Players from Iran’s national football team are in Turkey applying for US visas for the 2026 World Cup.

item.title: Who are the 10 best FIFA World Cup players of all time?
item.description: The all-time greats who have shone on the biggest stage and written their names in football World Cup folklore.

item.title: Poll finds nearly three-quarters of Democratic voters oppose aid to Israel
item.description: The US ally has seen support plummet in recent years amid widespread anger over its genocidal war on Gaza.

item.title: Displaced Lebanese woman bakes 3,000 loaves daily for war-hit community
item.description: Soubhiye Zeiter vowed to feed people affected by war even after being displaced from her home.

item.title: Net migration to the UK falls by nearly 50 percent amid tighter policies
item.description: The ONS says net migration fell to 171,000 in the ⁠12 months to the end of December from 331,000 a year earlier.

item.title: Mayor cuts limited World Cup tickets for New Yorkers to $50 after furore
item.description: Pricing for 2026 World Cup has been under heavy scrutiny, including in New York where city mayor cuts limited tickets.

item.title: Shocked Maguire posts on Insta that he is not in England’s World Cup squad
item.description: Manchester United defender Harry Maguire has announced on social media that he is not going to World Cup 2026.

item.title: Top UN court rules workers’ right to strike ‘protected’ in key treaty
item.description: The nonbinding ruling is expected to be hailed as a victory by workers' groups and influence global labour relations.

item.title: French Open rejects prize money change despite tennis stars’ boycott threat
item.description: Top-ranked Aryna Sabalenka and No 4 Coco Gauff have led player complaints about reduced pay at French Open.

item.title: ‘Lingering shock from tragedy’ weeks after Minab school bombing
item.description: Residents are calling for justice weeks after US missiles killed at least 156 people at a school in Minab, Iran.

item.title: Trump says he’ll speak to Taiwan’s leader: Why that is significant
item.description: Analysts say the remarks are less a sign of closer US-Taiwan ties than another example of Trump’s unpredictability.

item.title: Why are India’s mosques turning into temple disputes?
item.description: India’s courts are reopening mosque disputes despite laws meant to protect religious sites. What changed?

item.title: Is the West de-risking from China or containing its economy?
item.description: As the US and Europe push to reduce reliance on China, Beijing tightens control over supply chains.

item.title: Putin and Lukashenko monitor joint Russia-Belarus nuclear exercises
item.description: Moscow and Minsk hold strategic nuclear drills with the two countries' leaders participating via video conference.

item.title: Afghan women’s refugee team returns to world cricket post Taliban exclusion
item.description: Afghanistan women's refugee team will tour United Kingdom in June and be guests at July's World Cup final at Lord's.

item.title: Russia rehearses ‘use of nuclear forces’ in drills with Belarus
item.description: Russia delivered nuclear warheads to sites in Belarus as part of a three-day military exercise involving both countries.

item.title: Ecuador’s Disappeared
item.description: Fault Lines investigates forced disappearances conducted by Ecuador’s military while waging a war against drug cartels.
